rationale: The US-Iran conflict with Strait of Hormuz closure continues with active exchanges (oil tanker struck, video of US warship firing), maintaining direct pressure on Polish energy prices — no qualitative escalation but conflict remains live and unresolved.

top_events.title: US-Iran strikes continue; oil tanker hit, Hormuz closed
top_events.summary: US and Iran exchanged strikes for a second consecutive day; a US strike killed three Indian sailors on an oil tanker, and Iran continues to close the Strait of Hormuz. The Hormuz closure directly threatens oil supply routes, keeping upward pressure on fuel and energy prices in Poland.

top_events.title: UN watchdog demands Iran disclose nuclear stockpile
top_events.summary: The IAEA formally demanded Iran provide information on its nuclear stockpile amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict. If Iran accelerates nuclear activity during the war, this raises the stakes of the conflict significantly — indirect but real concern for European security.

top_events.title: Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang to revitalise China-North Korea ties
top_events.summary: Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang to strengthen China-North Korea relations. This strategic alignment could complicate NATO posture and US focus, with indirect implications for Poland's security environment — no immediate change.
